properties.titulo_evento: Zelenskyy taps European allies to build Freya, a cheaper Patriot-alternative to Russia’s ballistic missiles
properties.descricao: Ukraine will hold its first coalition meeting on a homegrown ballistic missile defense system in France “in the coming days.”

properties.titulo_evento: Italy busts Russian spy ring collecting data on Ukrainian air defense vulnerabilities
properties.descricao: Russia has told one of its spies in Europe to find out more about air defense capabilities Western nations are sending to Ukraine.

properties.titulo_evento: UK eyes $50 billion in pooled NATO funds for new long-range strike initiative
properties.descricao: Details were scant about a project that may amount to another European weapons-coordination umbrella rather than new technology development.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany to buy US Tomahawks in shift toward own long-range capability
properties.descricao: Germany will purchase Tomahawk cruise missiles from the United States and station them on German soil.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war live: US demands Iran publicly state Strait of Hormuz open for all
properties.descricao: US said to be pushing for public statement that Iran will not fire at commercial ships passing through Strait of Hormuz

properties.titulo_evento: Cuba suffers second island-wide blackout in a week amid Trump fuel blockade
properties.descricao: US President Donald Trump has placed the Caribbean island under a de facto oil blockade, straining its power grid.

properties.titulo_evento: Under Lula, Amazon deforestation falls to lowest level in a decade
properties.descricao: The figures come months before Brazil’s presidential election, with Lula promising to end illegal deforestation by 2030.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukrainian attacks cause chaos at fuel stations across Russia
properties.descricao: Ukraine’s targeting of Russia’s major oil refineries has sparked a nationwide fuel shortage.

properties.titulo_evento: Can the agreement between Iran and the US be rescued?
properties.descricao: Latest attacks jeopardise ceasefire and memorandum of understanding.

properties.titulo_evento: China lands reusable rocket for first time, state media says
properties.descricao: It follows similar landings of reusable rockets by US-owned companies SpaceX and Blue Origin.

properties.titulo_evento: At Canada's biggest rodeo, the starting gun is fired in the fight over Alberta separation
properties.descricao: Canadian unity takes centre stage at the Calgary Stampede as fears of a Brexit-style upset looms over the October vote on Alberta's future.

properties.titulo_evento: What's next for Iran after the funeral of their late supreme leader?
properties.descricao: The BBC's chief international correspondent looks at how Iran's new leadership will respond to several issues affecting the country.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran: Forewarnings and a Funeral, NATO, Le Pen

properties.titulo_evento: Cuba suffers second nationwide blackout in five days
properties.descricao: Cuba's electricity grid has crumbled amid a six-month US fuel blockade and already dilapidated energy infrastructure.

properties.titulo_evento: Mayor of Berlin says he won't run for reelection
properties.descricao: Berlin Mayor Kai Wegner withdrew from the reelection race this fall. His decision to play tennis during the power outage in January was among missteps that has dented his reputation.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump signals support for Patriot production in Ukraine
properties.descricao: US President Donald Trump says he may let Ukraine produce Patriot air defense missiles. Analysts welcome the move as a sign of support for the country — but warn it could take years before production begins.

properties.titulo_evento: Passenger partly sucked from Ryanair plane during flight
properties.descricao: A man was nearly sucked out the cabin window on a Ryanair flight from Greece to Germany. An initial loud bang was followed by a desperate rescue effort to save the passenger.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
properties.descricao: Dieter Schwarz made his fortune from supermarkets, becoming Germany's wealthiest person. Now he wants to take on Google, Microsoft and Amazon and an entire region stands to benefit.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukrainian drone strikes ignite fires at Russian oil sites
properties.descricao: An overnight assault reached key oil facilities in southern Russia, adding pressure to a fuel network already strained by repeated Ukrainian strikes.

properties.titulo_evento: Violence on trains in Germany is on the rise
properties.descricao: The verdict in the trial over the killing of a train conductor highlights growing aggression toward railway employees in Germany. What is causing it — and what can be done about it?

properties.titulo_evento: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
properties.descricao: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

properties.titulo_evento: Iran and US to continue talks, but ceasefire is over, Trump says
properties.descricao: The deal stopping hostilities is over “in no uncertain terms,” Trump said Friday.

properties.titulo_evento: Berlin mayor drops reelection bid over blackout lie
properties.descricao: Pressure on the conservative politician has mounted over the past week following media reports alleging he had lied to the public during a blackout in January.

properties.titulo_evento: Spain shrugs off Trump’s claim it caved on defense spending
properties.descricao: Spanish officials expressed confusion after the U.S. president said Madrid had "honored" his military expenditure demands.

properties.titulo_evento: Zelenskyy: China urged Putin not to use nuclear weapons in Ukraine
properties.descricao: The Ukrainian president said he learned of Beijing's intervention from European leaders at the NATO summit in Ankara.

properties.titulo_evento: Crude Oil Prices Slump on Limited Escalation of US-Iran Conflict
How Volkswagen’s Troubles Were Made in China
properties.descricao: The German auto giant’s push into China powered it for decades, but now the company faces fierce competition from Chinese automakers in markets around the world.

properties.titulo_evento: Renewed Fighting With Iran Shows Cracks in Peace-Trade Rally
properties.descricao: Investors have taken heart from the fragile cease-fire between the United States and Iran before. Will they do so again?

properties.titulo_evento: Help! I Refueled My Rental Car, but Enterprise Charged Me Anyway.
properties.descricao: A traveler to Germany returned his vehicle with slightly more gas than it had when he picked it up. So why was he billed $131 to fill up the tank?
